zh.North Weston, Oxfordshire, England: 英國英格蘭奧可福爾諾斯 韋斯頓
zh.lifeSummary: Herbert Quartermain是Herbert Quartermain的兒子，於1160年之前出生在英國英格蘭奧可福爾諾斯 韋斯頓。他於1225年與Lecia Knyvet在英國英格蘭奧可福爾諾斯 韋斯頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1201年9月之前在英國英格蘭奧可福爾。

nameMeanings.name: Quartermain
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Quarterman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Germaine, Marcel, Pascal.

English (Oxfordshire and Surrey): nickname from Anglo-Norman French quatre mayns, quatre mans ‘four hands’, which has been taken to imply ‘mail-fisted’ but was perhaps used of a skilled craftsman or a dextrous juggler or conjuror.

nameMeanings.name: Herbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It is derived from heri, hari ‘army’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. An Old English form, Herebeorht, existed in England before the Conquest, but was superseded by the Norman form, which gave rise to an important surname. The family in question were earls of Pembroke in the 16th and 17th centuries; they included the poet George Herbert . By the end of the Middle Ages Herbert was little used, although it remained a favourite with some families, notably the Saint Quintins of East Yorkshire. Its greater frequency in Britain from the 19th century onwards is due partly to the trend for the revival of medieval names of Germanic origin and partly to the trend for the transferred use of surnames.

variants.name: Heribert
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It is derived from heri, hari ‘army’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. An Old English form, Herebeorht, existed in England before the Conquest, but was superseded by the Norman form, which gave rise to an important surname. The family in question were earls of Pembroke in the 16th and 17th centuries; they included the poet George Herbert . By the end of the Middle Ages Herbert was little used, although it remained a favourite with some families, notably the Saint Quintins of East Yorkshire. Its greater frequency in Britain from the 19th century onwards is due partly to the trend for the revival of medieval names of Germanic origin and partly to the trend for the transferred use of surnames.

en.lifeSummary: Herbert Quartermain was born before 1160, in North Weston, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom as the son of Herbert Quartermain. He married Lecia Knyvet in 1225, in North Weston, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died before September 1201, in Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.North Weston, Oxfordshire, England: イギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアノース ウエストン
ja.lifeSummary: Herbert Quartermainは1160年以前にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアノース ウエストンでHerbert Quartermainの息子として生まれた。1225年にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアノース ウエストンでLecia Knyvetと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1201年9月以前にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Herbert Quartermain(은)는 1160년 이전 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 노르스 웨스턴에서 Herbert Quartermain의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1225년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 노르스 웨스턴에서 Lecia Knyvet(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1201년 9월 이전 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Herbert Quartermain родился перед 1160 г. в местности норт вестон, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. Его отца звали Herbert Quartermain. Он женился на женщине по имени Lecia Knyvet в 1225 г. в местности норт вестон, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер перед сентябрь 1201 г. в местности оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания.
